skill.name: coingecko
skill.description: CoinGecko API reference for cryptocurrency prices, market data, exchanges, NFTs, on-chain DEX endpoints, authentication, and SDK integration guidance.
skill.summary: Use CoinGecko API documentation to build crypto market data integrations.
skill.author: 2025Emma

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, credential, and filesystem patterns. Reviewed evidence shows these are Markdown documentation, API URLs, endpoint placeholders, and code examples. No malicious execution path or exfiltration intent was found, but API-key URL examples and embedded assistant prompts need user caution.

medium_findings.title: API Key Query String Examples
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes query-string API key examples. This is a real credential exposure risk if users copy the pattern with live keys, because URLs can appear in logs, browser history, and proxies. The same section recommends header authentication, so this is a content risk rather than malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly show API key query parameters. The surrounding documentation also warns that query strings can expose keys, which confirms the risk context.

medium_findings.title: Embedded Assistant Prompt Guidance
medium_findings.description: The reference material contains prompt-style rules for AI coding assistants. The rules focus on using the official SDK and safer credential handling, but they could steer an assistant if injected wholesale into unrelated tasks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are clearly written as assistant instructions with ALWAYS and NEVER language. I did not find evidence that they override security review or claim special authority.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Backticks and curl examples triggered command execution rules. The reviewed files are Markdown reference content, not executable scripts, and no code path runs these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are prose, inline code formatting, or API documentation examples. No executable shell, Ruby, or helper script is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Expected API Documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to CoinGecko API endpoints, documentation pages, images, package repositories, and support links. This is expected for an API documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references and API examples. I found no hidden endpoint or unauthorized network call made by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Traversal Alerts Are Endpoint Placeholders
low_findings.description: Path traversal alerts are caused by CoinGecko endpoint notation using double dots as placeholders. These are API path templates inside tables, not local filesystem operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequences appear in documented REST endpoint paths. There is no file read, file write, path join, or filesystem API usage in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Secret Access Findings Are Safe Credential Handling Examples
low_findings.description: Environment variable references demonstrate how to load CoinGecko API keys. This is legitimate documentation, though users still need to protect their own secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable access appears inside SDK sample code and recommends avoiding hardcoded keys. It does not collect or transmit local environment variables by itself.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Examples With Credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference content shows CoinGecko API calls that include placeholder API keys in URLs or headers. Users should prefer headers and avoid storing real keys in URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly contain API key parameter names and placeholder values. The risk depends on user copying behavior, not on autonomous skill execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt-Style Reference Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes AI assistant rules for CoinGecko SDK usage. This should be treated as untrusted reference content during security review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The block is explicitly a prompt for coding assistants. It lacks malicious override phrases, but its format can influence model behavior.

content.value_statement: Crypto applications need reliable market data and clear endpoint guidance. This skill helps you choose CoinGecko APIs, authentication methods, and SDK patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains CoinGecko Demo and Pro API authentication methods.
Maps endpoints for coins, contracts, exchanges, NFTs, trending data, and global markets.
Provides request parameter guidance for price, market, history, OHLC, and token data.
Summarizes official SDK usage patterns for TypeScript and other supported examples.
Helps troubleshoot rate limits, usage credits, API keys, and endpoint selection.
limitations: It does not call the CoinGecko API by itself.
Captured documentation can lag behind current CoinGecko API changes.
Users must provide and protect their own API keys.
It is not financial, trading, tax, or investment advice.

use_cases.title: Create a Price Tracker
use_cases.description: Find the right endpoints and parameters for live cryptocurrency price, market cap, and volume data.

use_cases.title: Integrate Pro API Authentication
use_cases.description: Compare header and query authentication methods, then implement safer API key handling.

use_cases.title: Research Token and Exchange Data
use_cases.description: Locate endpoint references for token contracts, exchanges, NFTs, trending assets, and on-chain pools.

prompt_templates.title: Find an Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Which CoinGecko endpoint should I use to get current Bitcoin and Ethereum prices in USD? Explain required parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up CoinGecko Pro API authentication using headers. Include security cautions for API key storage.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Market Data Calls
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CoinGecko data flow for a portfolio dashboard with prices, market charts, and exchange tickers.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my CoinGecko integration plan for rate limits, endpoint selection, retry handling, and secure key usage.

output_examples.input: I need prices for several coins in USD and EUR.
output_examples.output: Use the simple price endpoint with coin IDs and both target currencies. Include cache timing, rate limits, and response field notes.

output_examples.input: I want token data by contract address.
output_examples.output: Use the token price or token metadata endpoints for the correct asset platform. Confirm the network before querying contract addresses.

output_examples.input: I need historical market data for a chart.
output_examples.output: Use market chart endpoints with the coin ID, target currency, and time range. Choose range endpoints for custom periods.

best_practices: Use header authentication instead of query-string API keys.
Confirm CoinGecko coin IDs before using symbols or names.
Cache responses and respect the plan rate limit.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in frontend code.
Do not assume token symbols are unique across networks.
Do not treat market data responses as trading recommendations.

faq.question: Does this skill make live API requests?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation guidance and examples. Your application or agent must make any API requests.

faq.question: Does it support CoinGecko Pro API keys?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Demo and Pro authentication guidance, including safer header-based key usage.

faq.question: Can it help with token contract data?
faq.answer: Yes. It references token price and token metadata endpoints by asset platform and contract address.

faq.question: Can I use it for NFT market data?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes CoinGecko NFT endpoint references for collections and contract-based lookups.

faq.question: Is the information always current?
faq.answer: No. The skill reflects captured documentation. Check CoinGecko docs for recent API changes.

faq.question: Does it provide financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It explains API usage and data access. It does not recommend trades or investments.
